perturbation.original_text: Base Servicing Fee Rate: As to any Mortgage Loan, the per annum rate specified to be payable to Freedom Mortgage to cover the actual costs of servicing. For example, the Base Servicing Fee Rate for the Mortgage Loans in the initial pool will be eight (8) basis points.
perturbation.changed_text: Base Servicing Fee Rate: As to any Mortgage Loan, the per annum rate specified to be payable to Freedom Mortgage to cover the actual costs of servicing. The Base Servicing Fee Rate for the Mortgage Loans will be determined by the parties at a later date.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the specific example of 'eight (8) basis points' to 'will be determined by the parties at a later date', the contract now lacks a concrete figure. This introduces uncertainty, especially if 'later date' is not defined or acted upon. The in-text contradiction arises in how the rate will be determined for the initial pool, since it mentions that the rate will be determined at a later date, therefore it creates uncertainty in how the initial Base Servicing Fee Rate will be determined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Base Servicing Fee: As to any Mortgage Loan and any Collection Period, an amount equal to the product of the Base Servicing Fee Rate, the UPB of that Mortgage Loan as of the related Measurement Date and 1/12 or, for the first Collection Period, the number of days in such Collection Period divided by 360; provided, however, that payment of the Base Servicing Fee for any delinquent Mortgage Loan shall be suspended unless and until Freedom Mortgage recovers the amount thereof from payments in respect thereof from the related mortgagor or the amount thereof is otherwise recovered from liquidation of the related property.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided a specific rate (eight basis points) for the Base Servicing Fee. The changed text removes this specific rate and states that it will be determined later. This contradicts the Contradicted Text, which uses 'Base Servicing Fee Rate' in a calculation. Without a defined rate, the calculation in the Contradicted Text becomes impossible to perform. The omission of a concrete rate creates an uncertainty and renders the contradicted section unenforceable as it relies on a rate that is no longer defined.

perturbation.original_text: (c) Cherry Hill agrees that upon the request of Freedom Mortgage, Cherry Hill shall cooperate with Freedom Mortgage's efforts to cause the Acknowledgement Agreement to be revised or replaced with an alternative arrangement proposed by Freedom Mortgage that is acceptable to Ginnie Mae and that will provide Cherry Hill with benefits, rights and remedies that are, in the reasonable judgment of Cherry Hill, not materially less favorable than those provided under the Acknowledgement Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) Cherry Hill agrees that upon the request of Freedom Mortgage, Cherry Hill shall cooperate with Freedom Mortgage's efforts to cause the Acknowledgement Agreement to be revised or replaced with an alternative arrangement proposed by Freedom Mortgage. The acceptability of the alternative arrangement to Ginnie Mae and the favorability of benefits, rights, and remedies to Cherry Hill will be discussed in good faith.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the condition that the alternative arrangement provides benefits, rights, and remedies not materially less favorable to Cherry Hill and also, the acceptability of that arrangement to Ginnie Mae, and replacing it with 'will be discussed in good faith,' introduces ambiguity. The removal of the 'not materially less favorable' standard creates an in-text contradiction with the initial purpose of the agreement, where the intent was to have benefits to both of the parties, that creates an in-text contradiction. The 'good faith' discussion is less enforceable than the previous standard.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, Cherry Hill will seek to benefit from having a consistent and predictable source of real estate assets from Freedom Mortgage, and Freedom Mortgage will seek to benefit from the liquidity available to Cherry Hill; and WHEREAS, the parties desire to set forth the terms of a strategic alliance that is expected to benefit them both; NOW, THEREFORE, in consideration of the foregoing, and of other good and valuable consideration, the receipt and sufficiency of which is hereby acknowledged, the parties here to agree as follows.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement that the alternative arrangement proposed by Freedom Mortgage be 'not materially less favorable' to Cherry Hill. This directly contradicts the contradicted text which states that both parties 'will seek to benefit' and that the strategic alliance is 'expected to benefit them both.' By removing the protection for Cherry Hill to receive benefits that are no less favorable, it creates a direct contradiction with the stated purpose and intent of the agreement, making it possible for Cherry Hill to enter an alternative arrangement that is not beneficial, therefore contradicting the 'benefit them both' statement.
